  3. › Portuguese Water Dog vs Spanish Water Dog

Portuguese Water Dog vs Spanish Water Dog — Complete Comparison

Choosing between the Portuguese Water Dog and the Spanish Water Dog comes down to lifestyle fit, living space, and the time you can commit to training and grooming. Both breeds are medium-sized dogs, so their space and feeding requirements are broadly similar. Both breeds have comparable exercise needs, though individual dogs will vary. Both breeds are well-suited to families with children.

Side-by-Side Comparison

Trait Portuguese Water Dog Spanish Water Dog
Size Medium Medium
Weight 16–25 kg 14–22 kg
Height 43–57 cm 40–50 cm
Lifespan 11–13 yrs 12–14 yrs
Exercise Need
Very High (5/5)
High (4/5)
Grooming Need
High (4/5)
Moderate (3/5)
Shedding
Very Low (1/5)
Very Low (1/5)
Trainability
Very High (5/5)
High (4/5)
Good with Children ✓ ✓
Good with Dogs ✓ ✓
Apartment Friendly ✗ ✗

Bold green indicates the better result for that trait. Exercise, grooming and shedding: lower is easier to manage. Trainability and lifespan: higher is better.

Health Comparison

Understanding each breed's health profile helps owners budget for insurance, schedule appropriate screening, and make informed decisions before purchasing.

Portuguese Water Dog (5 conditions)

  • hip dysplasia
  • progressive retinal atrophy
  • storage disease
  • juvenile dilated cardiomyopathy
  • alopecia

Spanish Water Dog (3 conditions)

  • hip dysplasia
  • progressive retinal atrophy
  • hypothyroidism

Conditions shared by both breeds: hip dysplasia , progressive retinal atrophy .

Which Breed Is Right for You?

Based on data across health, exercise, trainability, and temperament, here is how the two breeds compare across common owner priorities:

Best for families with children both Portuguese Water Dog and Spanish Water Dog
Best for apartment living neither breed — both thrive with access to outdoor space
Best for active owners Portuguese Water Dog (exercise level 5/5)
Best for first-time owners Portuguese Water Dog
Longer average lifespan Spanish Water Dog (12–14 yrs vs 11–13 yrs)

Explore Each Breed in Depth

Portuguese Water Dog

The Portuguese Water Dog was bred along the Algarve coast of Portugal to work alongside fishermen, herding fish into net…

medium · Working · 11–13 yrs

Spanish Water Dog

The Spanish Water Dog is a versatile working breed from the Iberian Peninsula, historically used for herding, hunting, a…

medium · Herding · 12–14 yrs

Frequently Asked Questions

Which is bigger — a Portuguese Water Dog or a Spanish Water Dog? +

The Portuguese Water Dog is the larger breed, typically weighing 16–25 kg compared to the Spanish Water Dog's 14–22 kg.

Which breed lives longer — Portuguese Water Dog or Spanish Water Dog? +

The Spanish Water Dog generally has a longer lifespan (12–14 years) compared to the Portuguese Water Dog (11–13 years). Smaller breeds typically outlive larger ones, and individual genetics, diet, and preventive care all play significant roles.

Are Portuguese Water Dogs or Spanish Water Dogs easier to train? +

The Portuguese Water Dog (trainability 5/5) is generally easier to train than the Spanish Water Dog (4/5). Portuguese Water Dogs tend to respond quickly to reward-based training, while Spanish Water Dogs may require more patience and consistency.

Which needs more grooming — Portuguese Water Dog or Spanish Water Dog? +

The Portuguese Water Dog requires considerably more grooming (4/5) than the Spanish Water Dog (3/5). The Portuguese Water Dog's curly or wavy single coat needs more frequent brushing and professional grooming to stay in good condition.

Which breed is better for families — Portuguese Water Dog or Spanish Water Dog? +

Both the Portuguese Water Dog and Spanish Water Dog are generally considered good family dogs and are typically patient and gentle with children. As always, early socialisation and teaching children how to interact respectfully with dogs are essential regardless of breed.

Do Portuguese Water Dogs and Spanish Water Dogs have the same health problems? +

Some health conditions are shared between both breeds, including hip dysplasia, progressive retinal atrophy. The Portuguese Water Dog has 5 documented conditions overall and the Spanish Water Dog has 3. Pet insurance is strongly recommended for both.
